   143	@receiver(trace_paths, sender=Cable)
   144	def update_connected_endpoints(instance, created, raw=False, **kwargs):
   145	    """
   146	    When a Cable is saved with new terminations, retrace any affected cable paths.
   147	    """
   148	    logger = logging.getLogger('netbox.dcim.cable')
   149	    if raw:
   150	        logger.debug(f"Skipping endpoint updates for imported cable {instance}")
   151	        return
   152	
   153	    # Update cable paths if new terminations have been set
   154	    if instance._terminations_modified:
   155	        a_terminations = []
   156	        b_terminations = []
   157	        # Note: instance.terminations.all() is not safe to use here as it might be stale
   158	        for t in CableTermination.objects.filter(cable=instance):
   159	            if t.cable_end == CableEndChoices.SIDE_A:
   160	                a_terminations.append(t.termination)
   161	            else:
   162	                b_terminations.append(t.termination)
   163	        for nodes in [a_terminations, b_terminations]:
   164	            # Examine type of first termination to determine object type (all must be the same)
   165	            if not nodes:
   166	                continue
   167	            if isinstance(nodes[0], PathEndpoint):
   168	                create_cablepaths(nodes)
   169	            else:
   170	                rebuild_paths(nodes)
   171	
   172	    # Update status of CablePaths if Cable status has been changed
   173	    elif instance.status != instance._orig_status:
   174	        if instance.status != LinkStatusChoices.STATUS_CONNECTED:
   175	            CablePath.objects.filter(_nodes__contains=instance).update(is_active=False)
   176	        else:
   177	            rebuild_paths([instance])

   180	@receiver(post_delete, sender=Cable)
   181	def retrace_cable_paths(instance, **kwargs):
   182	    """
   183	    When a Cable is deleted, check for and update its connected endpoints
   184	    """
   185	    for cablepath in CablePath.objects.filter(_nodes__contains=instance):
   186	        cablepath.retrace()
   187	
   188	
   189	@receiver((post_delete, post_save), sender=PortMapping)
   190	def update_passthrough_port_paths(instance, **kwargs):
   191	    """
   192	    When a PortMapping is created or deleted, retrace any CablePaths which traverse its front and/or rear ports.
   193	    """
   194	    for cablepath in CablePath.objects.filter(
   195	        Q(_nodes__contains=instance.front_port) | Q(_nodes__contains=instance.rear_port)
   196	    ):
   197	        cablepath.retrace()
   198	
   199	
   200	@receiver(post_delete, sender=CableTermination)
   201	def nullify_connected_endpoints(instance, **kwargs):
   202	    """
   203	    Disassociate the Cable from the termination object, and retrace any affected CablePaths.
   204	    """
   205	    model = instance.termination_type.model_class()
   206	    model.objects.filter(pk=instance.termination_id).update(cable=None, cable_end='')
   207	
   208	    # If the parent Cable is being deleted in this same operation, skip the
   209	    # per-termination retrace; retrace_cable_paths() will retrace each affected
   210	    # path once after the Cable is deleted.
   211	    if Cable._is_being_deleted(instance.cable_id):
   212	        return
   213	
   214	    for cablepath in CablePath.objects.filter(_nodes__contains=instance.cable):
   215	        # Remove the deleted CableTermination if it's one of the path's originating nodes
   216	        if instance.termination in cablepath.origins:
   217	            cablepath.origins.remove(instance.termination)
   218	            # Clear _path on the removed origin to prevent stale connection display
   219	            model.objects.filter(pk=instance.termination_id, _path=cablepath.pk).update(_path=None)
   220	        cablepath.retrace()
   221	
   222	
   223	@receiver(post_save, sender=Interface)
   224	@receiver(post_save, sender=VMInterface)
   225	def update_mac_address_interface(instance, created, raw, **kwargs):
   226	    """
   227	    When creating a new Interface or VMInterface, check whether a MACAddress has been designated as its primary. If so,
   228	    assign the MACAddress to the interface.
   229	    """
   230	    if created and not raw and instance.primary_mac_address:
   231	        instance.primary_mac_address.assigned_object = instance
   232	        instance.primary_mac_address.save()
   233	
   234	
   235	@receiver(post_save, sender=Location)
   236	@receiver(post_save, sender=Site)
   237	def sync_cached_scope_fields(instance, created, **kwargs):
   238	    """
   239	    Rebuild cached scope fields for all CachedScopeMixin-based models
   240	    affected by a change in a Region, SiteGroup, Site, or Location.
   241	
   242	    This method is safe to run for objects created in the past and does
   243	    not rely on incremental updates. Cached fields are recomputed from
   244	    authoritative relationships.
   245	    """
   246	    if created:
   247	        return
   248	
   249	    if isinstance(instance, Location):
   250	        filters = {'_location': instance}
   251	    elif isinstance(instance, Site):
   252	        filters = {'_site': instance}
   253	    else:
   254	        return
   255	
   256	    # These models are explicitly listed because they all subclass CachedScopeMixin
   257	    # and therefore require their cached scope fields to be recomputed.
   258	    for model in (Prefix, Cluster, WirelessLAN):
   259	        qs = model.objects.filter(**filters)
   260

   488	        """
   489	        Return a list of rack units as dictionaries. Example: {'device': None, 'face': 0, 'id': 48, 'name': 'U48'}
   490	        Each key 'device' is either a Device or None. By default, multi-U devices are repeated for each U they occupy.
   491	
   492	        :param face: Rack face (front or rear)
   493	        :param user: User instance to be used for evaluating device view permissions. If None, all devices
   494	            will be included.
   495	        :param exclude: PK of a Device to exclude (optional); helpful when relocating a Device within a Rack
   496	        :param expand_devices: When True, all units that a device occupies will be listed with each containing a
   497	            reference to the device. When False, only the bottom most unit for a device is included and that unit
   498	            contains a height attribute for the device
   499	        """

  "step1_orientation": {
    "project": "NetBox is a Django network source-of-truth application organized as apps under netbox/: dcim, ipam, virtualization, circuits, wireless, vpn, extras, tenancy, users, core, utilities, and the netbox core package.",
    "core_model_location": "netbox/dcim/models/devices.py:498",
    "map": [
      "DCIM owns physical infrastructure models, views, API, GraphQL, forms, filters, tables, signals, rack/cable/device logic.",
      "IPAM owns IP addresses, VLANs, services, FHRP, and the primary/OOB IP interactions with device interfaces.",
      "Virtualization owns clusters and VMs; VMs may be pinned to devices and clusters count/assign devices.",
      "Wireless/VPN/circuits expose device-derived interface context through their own records and UI.",
      "Extras supplies config context/config template logic for device-like objects."
    ]
